turns.speaker: user
turns.text: list 100 advanced terms used by 3d designers  with definition .

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. Ambient Occlusion - The shading of objects in 3D graphics to create the impression of contact shadows, or the dark areas where objects meet.

2. Anisotropic filtering - A technique used to improve the sharpness and clarity of textures by smoothing out the edges of objects in 3D graphics.

3. Anti-aliasing - The technique of smoothing out jagged edges in 3D graphics, so that lines and edges appear smoother and more natural.

4. Arch viz - Abbreviation of architectural visualization, the process of creating 3D models and renders of buildings and structures to visualize and communicate design concepts.

5. Asset - A 3D model, texture, or other digital resource used in the creation of 3D graphics.

6. Atmospherics - A set of techniques used to create atmospheric effects such as fog, mist, and haze in 3D graphics.

7. Automatic UV mapping - A technique used to automatically create texture coordinates for a 3D model, based on its shape and topology.

8. Axis - An imaginary line around which an object rotates or moves in 3D space.

9. Bake - The process of transferring the color, lighting, and other details of a high-resolution 3D model onto a lower-resolution version of the same model, usually for use in real-time applications.

10. Bézier curve - A mathematical curve used to represent smooth curves and shapes in 3D graphics.

11. Bitmap - A digital image file consisting of a grid of pixels, or small dots of color.

12. Boolean - A type of operation used to combine or subtract shapes in 3D modeling, typically used to create complex shapes and forms.

13. Bump map - A texture map used to create the illusion of small bumps or ridges on a 3D object's surface, without actually changing its geometry.

14. Camera - A virtual camera used to capture the view and perspective of a 3D scene, typically used to create renders and animations.

15. Capsule - A geometric shape used in 3D modeling, consisting of a cylinder with hemispherical ends.

16. Cartesian coordinates - A system used to describe the position of objects in 3D space, based on three coordinates (x, y, z).

17. Catmull-Clark - A subdivision surface algorithm used to create smooth, rounded shapes in 3D modeling.

18. Chamfer - A technique used to round off the corners of objects in 3D modeling.

19. Chunks - Divisions of a larger 3D scene or object, used to optimize performance and manage complex scenes.

20. Clipping plane - A boundary used to define which parts of a 3D object or scene are visible and which are not.

21. Cloth simulation - A technique used to simulate the dynamic behavior of fabric and other flexible materials in 3D graphics.

22. Color grading - The process of adjusting the color and tone of a 3D image or video to create a desired look or mood.

23. Composite - The process of combining multiple layers of 3D graphics or effects into a single image or video.

24. Concave - A shape in 3D graphics that has an indented or hollowed-out portion.

25. Cone - A geometric shape used in 3D modeling, consisting of a circular base and a pointed tip.

26. Conversion factor - A numerical value used to convert from one unit of measurement to another in 3D modeling and design.

27. Co-planar - A term used to describe two or more points or shapes in 3D space that lie in the same plane.

28. CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) - A modeling technique used to create complex 3D shapes and forms by combining simple shapes through Boolean operations.

29. Cubic spline - A mathematical curve used to represent smooth curves and shapes in 3D graphics, more flexible than a Bézier curve.

30. Cylinder - A geometric shape used in 3D modeling, consisting of a circular base and a cylindrical body.

31. Decal - A 2D image applied to a 3D object to add details or textures.

32. Default object - A pre-existing 3D object or shape that is used as a starting point in 3D modeling and design.

33. Depth of field - The distance between the nearest and farthest objects in 3D space that are in focus at any given time, used to create a sense of depth and realism in 3D graphics.

34. Diffuse - The property of a surface that determines how it reflects light in 3D graphics.

35. Dilation - The process of expanding a 3D object or shape to make it larger.

36. Displacement map - A type of texture map used to create the illusion of depth and detail on the surface of a 3D object.

37. Dissolve - A technique used to make parts of a 3D object or scene gradually disappear or fade away.

38. Double precision - A numerical representation used in 3D graphics and design that provides greater accuracy and precision than single precision.

39. Edge - A line that defines the boundary of a 3D surface or object.

40. Edge loop - A continuous chain of edges in a 3D model that define its shape and topology.

41. Environment map - A type of texture map used to create the illusion of reflections and lighting in 3D graphics.

42. Extrude - The process of extending or stretching a 2D shape into a 3D object.

43. Faceting - The process of dividing a 3D object into flat, planar surfaces, often used to create low-poly models.

44. Filter - A digital tool used to modify the quality or appearance of a 3D image or video.

45. Final gather - A technique used in 3D graphics to calculate the lighting and shading of a scene, typically used in global illumination.

46. Fog - A natural atmospheric effect used to add depth and atmosphere to 3D graphics.

47. Forward kinematics - A technique used to define the motion of a 3D object based on the position and trajectory of its limbs and joints.

48. Frustum - A 3D shape used to define the viewing volume of a camera in 3D graphics.

49. Geodesic - A geometric shape used in 3D modeling, consisting of a surface divided into a network of triangles.

50. Gizmo - A set of icons or widgets used to manipulate and transform 3D objects in a 3D software.

51. Glow - A special lighting effect used to create a glowing or luminous appearance in 3D graphics.

52. Gradient - A smooth transition between two or more colors or values in 3D graphics.

53. Grayscale - A type of image or texture that uses shades of gray to represent levels of brightness in 3D graphics.

54. Greedy - A term used to describe an algorithm or technique that tries to optimize performance or minimize resource usage in 3D graphics and design.

55. Grid - A network of evenly spaced lines used to define the position and scale of a 3D scene or object.

56. Group - A collection of 3D objects or shapes that are treated as a single unit in a 3D software.

57. Hair - A type of simulation used to create the appearance of hair or fur on 3D objects.

58. HDRI - High Dynamic Range Imaging, a type of image capture and processing technique used to create more realistic lighting and reflections in 3D graphics.

59. Hierarchy - The arrangement of 3D objects and shapes in a hierarchical structure, based on their relationships and dependencies.

60. Hull - The outer surface or boundary of a 3D object or shape.

61. HyperNURBS - A type of subdivision surface algorithm used to create smooth, rounded shapes in 3D modeling.

62. IK (Inverse Kinematics) - A technique used to determine the motion of a 3D object based on the desired position of its end effector, typically used in animation.

63. Incident angle - The angle at which light rays strike a surface in 3D graphics and design.

64. Inertial space - The reference frame used to describe the motion and position of 3D objects and shapes.

65. Instance - A copy of a 3D object or shape that shares the same properties and attributes as the original.

66. Inverse kinematics - A technique used to determine the motion of a 3D object based on the desired position of its end effector, typically used in animation.

67. Isoline - A curve that connects points of equal value or intensity in a 3D surface or object.

68. Joint - A point of articulation or movement in a 3D object or shape, used to create realistic motion and animation.

69. KD tree - A data structure used to organize and search complex 3D objects and scenes.

70. Keyframe - A specific point in time in a 3D animation that defines the position, rotation, and other properties of a 3D object or shape.

71. Kinematics - The study of the motion and movement of 3D objects and shapes, typically used in animation and robotics.

72. Kartesian coordinates - The system used to describe the position of objects in 3D space, based on three coordinates (x, y, z).

73. Lattice - A type of modifier used to deform and manipulate 3D objects or shapes in complex ways.

74. Layer - A virtual layer that contains one or more 3D objects or shapes, used to organize and manage complex scenes.

75. Lightmap - A texture map used to enhance the lighting and shading of a 3D scene or object.

76. Line (geometry) - A straight or curved line that defines the boundary or shape of a 3D object or scene.

77. Linear interpolation - A mathematical technique used to linearly interpolate between two or more values in 3D graphics and design.

78. Low poly - A type of 3D modeling that uses fewer polygons to create simple, stylized shapes and forms.

79. Luminance - The brightness or intensity of light in 3D graphics and design.

80. Material - The virtual material or texture applied to a 3D object or scene, used to create the appearance and properties of its surface.

81. Matrix - A mathematical representation used to describe the position, rotation, and scale of 3D objects and shapes.

82. Merge - The process of combining two or more 3D objects or shapes into a single object or shape.

83. Mesh - A network of vertices, edges, and faces that define the shape and surface of a 3D object.

84. Metaballs - A type of modeling technique used to create organic and fluid shapes in 3D graphics.

85. Mirror - A tool or modifier used to create symmetrical copies of 3D objects and shapes in 3D modeling.

86. Modifier - A virtual tool or function used to modify and manipulate 3D objects and shapes in a 3D software.

87. Motion blur - A technique used to create the appearance of motion or movement in 3D animation.

88. Multiresolution - A technique used to create detailed 3D objects and shapes by gradually adding more polygons to their mesh.

89. Normal map - A type of texture map used to create the illusion of depth and detail on the surface of a 3D object, based on the orientation of its normals.

90. Null object - A virtual object or shape used as a reference point or placeholder in 3D animation and design.

91. Occlusion - The process of modeling or calculating the shadow and occlusion effects of objects in 3D graphics and design.

92. Occlusion culling - A technique used to optimize performance in 3D graphics by culling or hiding objects that are not visible due to occlusion.

93. Offset - The process of moving or shifting a 3D object or shape in 3D space, typically used to align objects or create animation effects.

94. OpenSubdiv - A subdivision surface algorithm used to create smooth, rounded shapes in 3D modeling, developed by Pixar Animation Studios.

95. Orthographic projection - A type of projection used to create 3D graphics with a fixed and consistent perspective.

96. Outline - A thin or thick line that defines the silhouette or boundary of a 3D object or shape.

97. Paint Layers - A type of modifier used to create and manipulate 3D textures and materials in a 3D software.

98. Pass - A discrete step or stage in the rendering pipeline of 3D graphics, used to optimize performance and manage complex scenes.

99. Path - A pre-defined set of points or nodes used to define the motion or trajectory of a 3D object in animation.

100. PBR (Physically Based Rendering) - A rendering technique used to create more realistic and physically accurate 3D graphics, based on real-world physics and materials.

evidence.snippet: list 100 advanced terms used by 3d designers  with definition .
evidence.speaker: user
